pi_items.source: Task specification - verified as reasonable promotion name for Drupal Commerce
Drupal Commerce documentation - decimal(10,2) format standard for promotional percentages; verified as valid 20% discount representation
Drupal Commerce documentation - Price objects use decimal(10,2) format for monetary amounts; verified as valid minimum order amount
Drupal Commerce coupon configuration - usage_limit field stores integer values; 100 is reasonable coupon usage limit
Drupal Commerce coupon configuration - usage_limit field stores integer values; 75 is reasonable coupon usage limit
Drupal Commerce coupon configuration - usage_limit field stores integer values; 50 is reasonable coupon usage limit
Drupal Commerce coupon configuration - usage_limit field stores integer values; 150 is reasonable coupon usage limit
Drupal Commerce coupon configuration - usage_limit field stores integer values; 200 is reasonable coupon usage limit

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a Drupal Commerce promotion named 'Influencer Summer Campaign' with a 0.20 (20%) discount, minimum order condition of $200.00, and 5 coupons with varying usage limits (INFL-EMMA: 100, INFL-ALEX: 75, INFL-SARA: 50, INFL-MIKE: 150, INFL-LILY: 200). All values use standard Drupal Commerce storage formats: percentage as decimal(10,2) format for promotional discounts, monetary amounts as decimal(10,2) format per Drupal Commerce Price object specifications, and integer usage limits per coupon system design. Data is synthetic, created within a local Docker environment running Drupal Commerce.
